questions.text: The uppermost layer over the earth's surface is called the:
questions.explanation: The crust is the thinnest of all the layers, about 35 km on continental masses.

questions.text: The depth of the crust on the ocean floors is only about:
questions.explanation: Oceanic crust is much thinner than continental crust.

questions.text: The main mineral constituents of the continental mass are:
questions.explanation: It is called sial (si-silica and al-alumina).

questions.text: The oceanic crust mainly consists of:
questions.explanation: Sima stands for silica (si) and magnesium (ma).

questions.text: Just beneath the crust is the ________ which extends up to a depth of 2900 km.
questions.explanation: The mantle lies between the crust and the core.

questions.text: The innermost layer of the earth is the core with a radius of about:
questions.explanation: The core is mainly made up of nickel and iron.

questions.text: The core is called 'nife' because it is made of:
questions.explanation: Ni stands for nickel and fe for ferrous (iron).

questions.text: The central core has very high:
questions.explanation: The extreme conditions at the center of the earth make it impossible to reach.

questions.text: Any natural mass of mineral matter that makes up the earth's crust is called a:
questions.explanation: Rocks can be of different colour, size, and texture.

questions.text: When molten magma cools, it becomes solid. Rocks thus formed are called:
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks are also called primary rocks.

questions.text: The two types of igneous rocks are:
questions.explanation: They are classified based on where the magma cools (inside or on the surface).

questions.text: Rocks formed when magma cools on the earth's surface are:
questions.explanation: Basalt is a classic example of an extrusive igneous rock.

questions.text: The Deccan plateau is made up of ________ rocks.
questions.explanation: Basaltic lava flows created the vast Deccan Traps in India.

questions.text: Sometimes the molten magma cools down deep inside the earth's crust. These are:
questions.explanation: Since they cool down slowly they form large grains, like granite.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of intrusive igneous rock?
questions.explanation: Granite is used for making grinding stones to prepare paste/powder of spices and grains.

questions.text: Rocks roll down, crack, and hit each other and are broken into small fragments called:
questions.explanation: These fragments are transported and deposited by wind and water.

questions.text: Rocks formed by the compression and hardening of layers of sediments are:
questions.explanation: Sandstone is made from grains of sand and is a sedimentary rock.

questions.text: Fossils are the remains of dead plants and animals found in:
questions.explanation: Sedimentary rocks trap organic remains between their layers.

questions.text: Igneous and sedimentary rocks can change into metamorphic rocks under great:
questions.explanation: Example: Clay changes into slate and limestone into marble.

questions.text: The Red Fort is made of:
questions.explanation: Red sandstone is a sedimentary rock used extensively in Indian architecture.

questions.text: The Taj Mahal is made of:
questions.explanation: White marble is a metamorphic rock formed from limestone.

questions.text: The process of transformation of the rock from one to another is known as the:
questions.explanation: It is a continuous process where one type of rock changes to another under certain conditions.

questions.text: Magma originates from the ________ of the earth.
questions.explanation: Extreme heat in the interior melts rocks to form magma.

questions.text: Gold, petroleum and coal are examples of:
questions.explanation: Minerals are naturally occurring substances with certain physical properties and chemical composition.

questions.text: The deepest mine in the world is located in:
questions.explanation: It is about 4 km deep and was dug in search for oil/gold.

questions.text: To reach the centre of the earth, you would have to dig a hole of ________ km depth.
questions.explanation: The radius of the earth is approximately 6371 km.

questions.text: The crust forms only ________ per cent of the volume of the earth.
questions.explanation: Mantle forms 84% and the core forms 15% of the earth's volume.

questions.text: Igneous is a Latin word 'ignis' meaning:
questions.explanation: It refers to the volcanic origin and heat associated with these rocks.

questions.text: Sedimentary is a Latin word 'sedimentum' meaning:
questions.explanation: It refers to the settling of particles to form layers.

questions.text: Metamorphic is a Greek word 'metamorphose' meaning:
questions.explanation: It describes how rocks change their appearance and properties.

questions.text: Molten magma that comes out on the earth's surface and rapidly cools is called:
questions.explanation: Lava is fiery red molten magma coming from the interior of the earth.

questions.text: Which rock is used for making grinding stones?
questions.explanation: Granite's hardness and texture make it ideal for grinding.

questions.text: The oceanic crust mainly consists of silica and:
questions.explanation: This is why it is called 'sima'.

questions.text: The thinnest layer of the earth is:
questions.explanation: Compared to the thousands of kilometers of mantle and core, the crust is like a thin skin.

questions.text: Sedimentary rocks are transformed into metamorphic rocks by:
questions.explanation: Example: Limestone turning into marble.

questions.text: What is the radius of the earth?
questions.explanation: This is the distance from the surface to the center.

questions.text: Which layer of the earth is in a liquid state?
questions.explanation: While the inner core is solid due to pressure, the outer core is molten.

questions.text: Minerals are very important to humankind because:
questions.explanation: Modern civilization depends heavily on various minerals for energy, construction, and technology.

questions.text: Basalt is a/an ________ igneous rock.
questions.explanation: It cools on the surface and has a fine-grained structure.

questions.text: Limestone changes into ________ during metamorphism.
questions.explanation: Marble is the metamorphic version of limestone.
